summary: Completed the current-mainline live replay for ADR 0153 by reconverging Vaultwarden after the same-day OpenBao and Windmill dependency replays, then verifying the private controller-only service path and its runtime dependencies against production state.

verification.observed: A direct probe to `http://127.0.0.1:8201/v1/sys/health` on `docker-runtime-lv3` returned `200` after the final ADR 0153 replay sequence, confirming the local OpenBao API was unsealed and answering again.

The production replay finished on 2026-03-26 with `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=151 changed=11 unreachable=0 failed=0`, `postgres-lv3 : ok=57 changed=1 unreachable=0 failed=0`, and `proxmox_florin : ok=35 changed=4 unreachable=0 failed=0` before the final Vaultwarden-only runtime replay.
A direct probe to `http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/version` on `docker-runtime-lv3` returned `CE v1.662.0` after the current-mainline replay.
`docker compose --file /opt/windmill/docker-compose.yml ps` showed `windmill_server`, `windmill_worker`, and `windmill_worker_native` all `Up` after the replay.

A controller-side probe bound to the local Tailscale address and sent through the Proxmox host proxy returned `200` for `https://vault.lv3.org/alive`, confirming the private Vaultwarden entrypoint was reachable after the runtime replay.

`docker ps` on `docker-runtime-lv3` showed `vaultwarden` healthy with `10.10.10.20:8222->8222/tcp`, and the controller-local bootstrap receipt under `.local/vaultwarden/bootstrap.json` remained populated after the final replay.

notes: The pre-replay recovery path required a manual `systemctl restart docker` on `docker-runtime-lv3` to restore the missing `nat` `DOCKER` chain so newly published Windmill ports could start again.
OpenBao had to be manually unsealed once on `docker-runtime-lv3` using the existing controller-local recovery material after an interrupted replay left the local API half-unsealed.

The separate-worktree Windmill database password had drifted from the canonical root checkout `.local` secret already mirrored in OpenBao; syncing the worktree secret back to the canonical value was required before the successful current-mainline replay.

Vaultwarden required additional current-mainline hardening during the same live-apply sequence: the admin-token hash generation path now runs with a TTY-aware `vaultwarden hash` invocation, the PostgreSQL password is URL-encoded in the compose file, and the controller-path verification falls back to a Tailscale-bound probe when the private hostname does not resolve locally.
